subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 22
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a trunk?
questions.explanation: Elephants use their long trunks for breathing, smelling, touching, grasping, and producing sound.
questions.hint: It is the largest land animal.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Italy?
questions.explanation: Rome is the capital of Italy and is famous for its ancient history and landmarks like the Colosseum.

questions.q: Which part of the plant makes the seeds?
questions.explanation: Flowers are the reproductive structures of plants, and they are responsible for creating seeds.
questions.hint: They are often colorful and have petals.

questions.q: What do we call an animal that eats both plants and meat?
questions.explanation: An omnivore is an organism that can eat and survive on both plant and animal matter.

questions.q: Which of these is a good source of protein for muscle growth?
questions.explanation: Chicken is a lean meat that is an excellent source of protein, which is vital for building and repairing muscles.
questions.hint: It is a type of meat.

questions.q: Which community helper helps put out fires?
questions.explanation: Firefighters are trained to suppress and extinguish fires to protect people and property.
questions.hint: They wear red helmets and ride big trucks.

questions.q: What is the name of the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is often called the Red Planet because of the iron oxide (rust) on its surface.
questions.hint: It comes after Earth in our solar system.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of energy that we get from the Sun?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is energy that is harvested from the light and heat of the Sun.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in degrees Fahrenheit?
questions.explanation: At sea level, water boils at 212 degrees Fahrenheit.

questions.q: Which season is known for snow and cold weather?
questions.explanation: Winter is the coldest season, often associated with snow, frost, and short days.
